title: He Built a Machine to Catch His Own Thieves
desc:
In 1878, Dayton saloon owner James Ritty suspected his bartenders were skimming cash — then a ship's propeller counter gave him an idea. He and his brother built a machine to record every sale, patented in 1879 as "The Incorruptible Cashier." It flopped, and Ritty sold out for about $1,000 — the company that grew from it became National Cash Register, worth billions.

#BrokeToBuilt #history #invention #NCR
